mechanism: Potent inhibitor of FLT3 receptor tyrosine kinase, blocks signaling from FLT3‑ITD mutants to inhibit proliferation
additional_notes: Approved for FLT3‑mutated AML; active against FLT3‑ITD models such as MOLM‑13. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Selective FLT3 kinase inhibitor with high potency against FLT3‑ITD
additional_notes: Also known as AC220; strong preclinical activity in FLT3‑ITD models. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits FLT3 and multiple kinases (including PKC), blocking oncogenic kinase signaling
additional_notes: Approved in combination for FLT3‑mutated AML; multi‑kinase activity may help in heterogeneous disease. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits multiple kinases including FLT3, RAF kinases, VEGFR, and PDGFR, reducing proliferation and survival signaling
additional_notes: Used off‑label and studied in FLT3‑ITD AML. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Broad-spectrum TKI inhibiting BCR‑ABL and other kinases including some FLT3 mutants, blocking oncogenic kinase activity
additional_notes: Investigated for resistant FLT3 variants. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Selective inhibitor of anti‑apoptotic BCL‑2, promoting mitochondrial apoptosis in leukemia cells
additional_notes: Approved in AML combinations; often synergizes with FLT3 inhibitors. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits nuclear export protein XPO1/CRM1, causing nuclear retention of tumor suppressors and pro‑apoptotic factors
additional_notes: Preclinical AML activity and early‑phase clinical investigation. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits thioredoxin reductase, inducing oxidative stress and apoptosis
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with broad preclinical antileukemic activity; multiple studies across AML cell lines. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Multiple reported effects including Wnt/STAT3 inhibition and mitochondrial uncoupling, leading to impaired survival pathways
additional_notes: Frequent repurposing candidate with reported AML cytotoxicity in cell line screens. Concentration range not provided in source text.

category: Aldehyde dehydrogenase inhibitor / copper‑dependent cytotoxic agent
mechanism: Inhibits ALDH and, when combined with copper, generates reactive species and can inhibit proteasome activity leading to cell death
additional_notes: Reported anticancer effects often in combination with copper. Preclinical AML cytotoxicity reported. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Irreversible inhibitor of Bruton's tyrosine kinase (BTK), modulating signaling pathways that can support AML cell survival in some subsets
additional_notes: BTK expressed in some AML subsets; tested in combinations and shows preclinical activity. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Selective BTK inhibitor with reduced off‑target activity compared with first‑generation BTK inhibitors
additional_notes: Similar rationale to ibrutinib with potentially fewer off‑target effects; preclinical AML interest. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits histone deacetylases (HDACs), altering chromatin and gene expression to restore apoptosis and inhibit proliferation
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA; investigated in AML preclinical studies and clinical trials, often in combinations. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Allosteric inhibitor of MEK1/2 in the MAPK pathway, reducing ERK signaling especially relevant in RAS‑mutated contexts
additional_notes: Potentially useful in NRAS/KRAS mutant AML; preclinical activity in RAS‑driven models. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1 and JAK2 kinases, blocking JAK/STAT signaling and cytokine‑driven proliferation/survival
additional_notes: Used for JAK/STAT pathway‑active AML subsets; investigated preclinically and clinically, often in combinations. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Alters iron metabolism and impairs ribonucleotide reductase and other iron‑dependent processes, leading to cytotoxicity
additional_notes: Repurposing candidate with reported preclinical AML cytotoxicity. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Inhibits HMG‑CoA reductase, affecting prenylation and cholesterol metabolism, which can induce pro‑apoptotic effects and synergize with other agents
additional_notes: Representative of statins studied in AML; preclinical studies and some clinical exploratory work. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Alters cellular metabolism and activates AMPK, leading to effects on proliferation and metabolic stress
additional_notes: Reported to limit leukemia cell proliferation in some preclinical contexts; interest in metabolic targeting. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Multiple proposed mechanisms including effects on dopamine receptors and reported selective targeting of leukemia stem‑like cells and impairment of self‑renewal
additional_notes: Reported to preferentially target leukemia stem cells in preclinical studies. Concentration range not provided in source text.

mechanism: Reversible inhibitor of the 26S proteasome, disrupting protein degradation and inducing apoptosis
additional_notes: Investigated clinically and preclinically in AML, often in combination regimens. Concentration range not provided in source text.
